It can be beneficial to use a guard, or covering, to protect your teeth from grinding while sleeping. Similar to how a mouthguard protects teeth during sports, this layer prevents damage to the teeth that can occur naturally. Typically these mouthguards cover your upper teeth only, but in certain instances, they are used for the lower teeth as well. Grinding teeth can be a serious problem that does major damage to your teeth, so wearing a nightguard is an effective way to prevent this damage.

4 _module_preset=default hover_enabled=0 global_colors_info={} sticky_enabled=0] Teeth grinding, clenching, and uneven bite balance can all take a toll on teeth and contribute to tooth damage and eventual tooth loss. As a top-ranked periodontist, Dr. Sunny K Tilwani offers state-of-the-art bite adjustments and night guards to help patients in Plano, TX, avoid tooth damage and chronic head and jaw pain.

Night guards are custom-fitted dental appliances worn while sleeping to prevent clenching and grinding. Both treatments ease pressure on your jaw and teeth and help you avoid long-term issues. A misaligned bite doesn’t always occur naturally – it can happen because of braces, missing teeth, dental implants, or even daily habits like grinding. Night guards help protect your teeth during sleep especially if you have bruxism which is the habit of grinding or clenching your teeth at night.

To learn more about mouthguards and how we can care for your teeth, please contact us today. What are Bite Adjustments and Night Guards. Bite adjustments and night guards are two different but related treatments to help your upper and lower teeth come together when you bite. Sometimes your teeth might not meet correctly and cause discomfort, pain, or damage over time. Bite adjustments focus on reshaping or fine-tuning the surface of your teeth to get a better alignment.

Because they’re custom fitted they’re comfortable and durable. Sports Mouth Guards A mouth guard isn’t just for nighttime – if you play contact sports a custom guard can protect your front teeth and jaw from impact. Unlike store-bought versions, ours are shaped specifically for your mouth. They stay in place better and offer stronger protection. TMJ Night Guards For patients with TMJ (temporomandibular joint) issues these guards help relieve pressure on the jaw.

This extends the life of your dental work and keeps your smile looking great. Helps With TMJ and Bruxism Symptoms Night guards and bite adjustments can reduce symptoms related to TMJ and bruxism. These include popping sounds in the jaw, jaw locking, and discomfort while chewing. Patients often feel better after just a few nights of using a night guard. The Bite Adjustments and Night Guards Process at Dr.

Bite Adjustments and Night Guards Getting bite adjustments and night guards in Plano can make a big difference in your daily comfort and long-term dental health. These treatments reduce stress on your jaw, protect your teeth from wear, and improve bite alignment. Many patients don’t even realize how much discomfort they are in until they get treatment. Whether you’re grinding at night or have sore gums and teeth during the day relief is possible.

Whether you’re experiencing discomfort from clenching at night or need to fine-tune your bite after braces we have a solution for you. We take the time to listen to your symptoms, evaluate your bite registrations, and develop a treatment plan that feels right for you. We want to relieve pain, improve function, and help you protect your smile for years to come. With a caring team and many treatment options, you’re in good hands here.

Occlusal Bite Adjustments These are minor reshaping procedures that adjust the surface of your teeth so they come together properly. This is especially helpful after getting new crowns, veneers, or dental implants. It helps balance your bite and reduce discomfort. Custom Night Guards These guards are made just for your mouth to protect your upper or lower teeth while you sleep. They prevent damage caused by grinding and clenching and reduce jaw pain in the morning.

By keeping the jaw slightly open and aligned during sleep they reduce pain and improve comfort. They also help with clenching and grinding. Post-Braces Bite Review and Adjustment After orthodontic treatment, your bite might need fine-tuning to avoid future problems. We can check your bite after braces and make minor adjustments to ensure proper alignment. This helps maintain the shape and comfort of your smile.

If left untreated these issues can cause problems like worn down teeth, jaw pain, or damage to dental work like crowns or veneers. Our Bite Adjustments and Night Guards Services At Dr. Sunny K Tilwani’s Plano office, we offer personalized bite adjustments and night guards for each patient.

This protects your smile and reduces the need for costly repairs. Improves Bite Alignment A misaligned bite can lead to long-term problems with eating, speaking, and comfort. Bite adjustments help create better contact between your teeth, and improve function. This can also reduce headaches and facial tension. Protects Dental Work If you’ve had dental implants, crowns, or veneers grinding can wear them down. Night guards protect these investments while you sleep.

With the right care, your mouth can feel more natural, balanced, and pain-free. Reduces Jaw Pain Constant clenching or grinding can cause serious tension in your jaw. Bite adjustments and night guards take pressure off the joints and muscles. This reduces pain and stiffness in the morning. Prevents Tooth Wear and Damage Grinding can wear down the surface of your teeth and cause cracks or chips. A night guard acts like a cushion between your upper and lower teeth.

We’ll review your bite registrations, examine the alignment of your upper and lower teeth, and ask about any discomfort you’re feeling. There are times when an antibiotic needs to be placed between your teeth and gums to help with the infection. What is Scaling and Root Planing. Scaling and root planing is a deep cleaning treatment to treat gum disease by removing plaque and tartar from below the gum line. While regular cleanings focus on the surface of the teeth, this procedure goes deeper to stop the infection and protect the gums and roots.

It’s especially helpful for people showing common signs of early or advanced periodontal disease like bleeding gums, bad breath, or gum recession. Scaling removes the hardened buildup called tartar, while root planing smooths out the root surfaces so the gums can reattach properly. The goal is to stop the bacteria from spreading and keep the mouth healthy. Over time plaque hardens into tartar and causes inflammation in the gums leading to gingivitis or periodontitis if not treated.

There are times when we use a local anesthetic to numb the area before performing the procedure. Be sure to tell your dentist if you would prefer local anesthesia before we start the procedure. From routine cleanings to more complex procedures, we’re here to make sure your dental experience is as calm and comfortable as possible. Our Sedation Dentistry Services We know every patient is different, so we offer several sedation dentistry options at our Plano office. Whether you’re nervous about a long procedure or have a severe fear of the dentist’s chair, our team will help you relax and feel at ease.

Sunny K Tilwani’s office we know that visiting the periodontist isn’t always easy. Whether it’s dental anxiety, fear of pain or a strong gag reflex, many patients put off important dental care simply because they are nervous. That’s why we offer sedation dentistry in Plano so you can get the care you need without the stress. Our sedation options are safe, effective and tailored to your needs.

You’ll be able to stay calm while still being aware of what’s going on. Makes Long Procedures Feel Shorter If your procedure will take several hours or involves multiple procedures, sedation dentistry makes the time fly by. Many patients even fall asleep or lose track of time entirely. This makes it easier to get more work done in fewer appointments.

Nitrous Oxide (Laughing Gas) This is one of the most common and gentle forms of sedation used in dentistry. It kicks in quickly and wears off just as fast, making it perfect for shorter treatments. You’ll feel calm and comfortable and you’ll be able to drive yourself home afterward. Local Anesthesia with Sedation We often use local anesthesia along with other sedation options to make it pain free. While the anesthesia numbs the area being treated, the sedation helps ease fear or anxiety.

This is perfect for longer or more involved dental work. Benefits of Sedation Dentistry Choosing sedation dentistry in Plano can be life changing for patients who have been putting off the periodontist for years. It allows you to get the dental care you need without the fear, stress or discomfort. At Dr. Sunny K Tilwani’s office, many patients tell us that sedation dentistry changed how they feel about the periodontist’s office.

Allows for More Dental Work in One Visit Sedation dentistry allows you to stay relaxed and still for longer periods, allowing us to get complete dental work done in one visit. That means fewer dental appointments and less time in the dental chair overall. It’s perfect for patients with busy schedules or dental backlogs. Helps with Gag Reflex and Sensitive Teeth Some patients have a strong gag reflex or sensitive teeth, making routine procedures unbearable.

Sedation dentistry helps you stay calm while still being awake and aware, allowing for a more pleasant experience. Our periodontist will go over your medical history, current health, and dental needs before recommending the best method. At Dr. Sunny K Tilwani’s practice your comfort and safety are always a top priority. Oral Conscious Sedation This involves taking a prescribed pill before your appointment. It helps you feel relaxed and sleepy, though you’ll still be awake during the entire time.

Many patients don’t even remember the procedure afterward, making it perfect for those with dental phobia or sensitive teeth. IV Sedation Dentistry IV sedation is a deeper form of dental sedation and is administered directly into the bloodstream. It allows the periodontist to control the level of sedation throughout your dental procedure. With IV sedation, many patients feel like they’re asleep, though they remain conscious.

Whether it’s oral sedation or IV sedation, our goal is to have you feel calm and confident every step of the way. If you’ve been putting off treatment due to anxiety or a strong gag reflex, sedation could be the answer. Reduces Fear and Anxiety Many people suffer from dental anxiety or even full dental phobia, making dental visits a nightmare. With conscious sedation you’ll feel relaxed and less overwhelmed during your appointment.

Sedation can calm your body’s natural reactions and make the process more comfortable. You’ll be able to sit through treatment without feeling overwhelmed. Creates a Positive Dental Experience For many patients, fear of discomfort prevents them from getting proper dental care. Sedation dentistry makes for a much more pleasant experience and helps patients come back with confidence. Over time it can change how you feel about the periodontist altogether. Our Wisdom Teeth Removal Services Dr. Sunny K Tilwani’s practice provides safe and effective wisdom teeth removal to patients of all ages. Whether you’re in pain, preparing for braces or just want to protect your overall oral health, we’re here to make the process smooth and stress free. It also protects against further gum recession. Bone Grafting Bone grafts are used when the jawbone has been damaged or lost due to advanced gum disease. Crown Lengthening This treatment reshapes the gum and bone levels to expose more of the tooth structure. It’s often used to place a crown or improve the appearance of your smile.

The goal is to remove bacteria, restore damaged tissue, and prevent tooth loss. Periodontal surgery rebuilds the foundation that keeps your teeth in place. It’s used for patients with gum recession, loose teeth, or deep pockets in the gum line. In some cases, it’s used to prep the gums and bone for other dental treatments like dental implants or crown lengthening. For example when there’s not enough healthy tissue to support an implant, soft tissue grafting may be done first.

Every patient is different, that’s why we customize your care plan from start to finish. Allograft and Xenograft These grafts use human or animal donor bone to restore bone tissue in the mouth. They’re safe, widely used and very effective for most types of bone grafting procedures. Sinus Lift When there’s not enough bone tissue in the upper jaw (especially near the back teeth), a sinus lift may be necessary. This adds height to the bone so implants can be placed securely. Sunny K Tilwani’s office believes every smile deserves a strong foundation. With advanced techniques and gentle care, Dr. Tilwani makes the whole process smooth and stress free. We want you to feel confident and informed every step of the way.

Our Bone Grafting Services Dr. Sunny K Tilwani’s office does bone grafting to help patients restore their jawbone health and prepare for procedures like dental implants. Whether it’s from injury, gum disease or tooth loss, bone grafting provides the structure to support replacement teeth. We customize every grafting procedure to your needs so you always get the best care. We use safe materials and gentle surgical procedures to promote healing and bone growth.

At our Plano office we’ve seen how life changing this simple surgical procedure can be for so many of our patients. Supports Dental Implants The most common reason for bone grafting is to prepare the jawbone for implant placement. Without enough bone, implants can’t anchor properly. A strong base ensures your new teeth stay secure and last longer. Prevents Bone Loss After tooth loss or extraction, the bone starts to shrink over time. A bone graft stops this process and even helps reverse it.

Dr. Sunny K Tilwani and his team will be with you every step of the way for bone grafting. Whether you’re getting dental implants or just want to preserve lost bone, we can help.

[/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title=Will I need a bone graft before getting dental implants. _builder_version=4. 27. 4 _module_preset=default open=on hover_enabled=0 sticky_enabled=0] If you’ve had tooth loss or a tooth extraction a while ago, you may need one. A strong jawbone is necessary to support dental implants properly. We’ll take X-rays and scans to determine if you have enough bone. If not bone grafting, it will give you the foundation you need.

[/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title=Where does the graft material come from. _builder_version=4. 27. 4 _module_preset=default open=on hover_enabled=0 sticky_enabled=0] We use several safe and proven sources for bone grafting material. It can come from a donor (human or animal) or your bone. Donor grafts are commonly used and are thoroughly sterilized and tested. We’ll explain your options and help you choose what’s best for your case.

Tilwani is committed to bring the latest techniques and procedures through continuing education and strives to provide highest quality periodontal care for all his patients. He truly believes each patient is unique and deserves an individualized treatment approach. Dr. Tilwani strongly believes in providing individualized, patient-centered care based on each patient’s unique needs for optimal outcomes. He welcomes new patients and the practice accepts most major dental insurance plans.

He graduated from Post-doctoral Periodontics and Implantology program at the prestigious Boston University.
